news.headline: Caribou Biosciences secures e-poster spot for ANTLER abstract at PPLC 2026
news.summary: An ANTLER Phase 1 trial abstract on vispa-cel in relapsed/refractory B-cell non-Hodgkin lymphoma was accepted as an e-poster at the 2026 Pan-Pacific Lymphoma Conference (PPLC), held July 20-24 in Hawaii. The presentation continues Caribou's pattern of showcasing incremental ANTLER follow-up data at hematology conferences following the EHA 2026 disclosures in June.

news.headline: Caribou Biosciences CEO Highlights Off-the-Shelf CAR-T Progress at H.C. Wainwright Cell Therapy Conference
news.summary: President and CEO Rachel Haurwitz participated in a fireside chat at the H.C. Wainwright 4th Annual Cell Therapy Virtual Conference, discussing Caribou's progress toward commercializing its allogeneic CAR-T platform. She pointed to the vispa-cel and CB-011 data presented at EHA in June as evidence the off-the-shelf approach can match autologous CAR-T efficacy.

news.headline: Caribou Biosciences Shareholders Decide Key Governance Matters at 2026 Annual Meeting
news.source: The Globe and Mail / Caribou Biosciences 8-K
news.summary: At its virtual annual meeting, Caribou shareholders elected three Class II directors (Andrew Guggenhime, David Johnson, and Nancy Whiting) and ratified Deloitte & Touche as auditor for fiscal 2026. Investors rejected a proposed charter amendment that would have expanded exculpation protections for company officers under Delaware law.

news.headline: Caribou Boosts Case That Off-the-Shelf CAR-T Matches Autologous Drugs Ahead of Phase 3
news.summary: Ahead of its EHA 2026 oral presentation, Caribou disclosed that vispa-cel achieved a 17.1-month median progression-free survival in second-line lymphoma patients, bolstering the case that its allogeneic therapy can rival autologous CAR-T. The planned pivotal ANTLER-3 Phase 3 trial will target patients who are ineligible for autologous CAR-T or cannot access it, such as those living too far from treatment centers.

news.headline: Caribou Reports Dose Escalation Durability Data for CB-011 at EHA 2026
news.summary: A single dose of CB-011 produced deep, durable responses in heavily pretreated, BCMA-naive relapsed/refractory multiple myeloma patients, with an 83% complete response rate, 91% MRD negativity, and 50% of patients still in complete response at 15 months. Data were presented in an oral session at the EHA 2026 Annual Meeting in Stockholm.

news.headline: Caribou Reports Long-Term Vispa-cel (CB-010) Data in Second-Line Large B-Cell Lymphoma at EHA 2026
news.summary: Updated ANTLER Phase 1 data showed vispa-cel (formerly CB-010) achieved an 82% overall response rate, 67% complete response rate, and 17.1-month median progression-free survival in an optimized 27-patient second-line LBCL subgroup, durability Caribou says is on par with autologous CAR-T therapies. The release also disclosed two vispa-cel-related or possibly related patient deaths in the trial, underscoring that safety remains a watch item alongside the efficacy data.

catalysts.label: CaMMouflage Phase 1 dose-expansion data for CB-011
catalysts.detail: Initial dose-expansion data from the CaMMouflage Phase 1 trial of CB-011 in BCMA-naive and BCMA-exposed relapsed/refractory multiple myeloma patients is expected in the second half of 2026, building on the dose-escalation durability data presented at EHA in June. CB-011 also carries FDA Regenerative Medicine Advanced Therapy (RMAT) designation, granted March 31, 2026.

catalysts.label: Q2 2026 earnings and business update
catalysts.detail: Caribou is expected to report second-quarter 2026 financial results and provide a pipeline update around August 11-12, 2026 per consensus earnings-calendar estimates, likely including further detail on ANTLER-3 trial start-up progress and updated cash-runway guidance; as of July 24 the company has still not formally confirmed the exact date via press release.

catalysts.label: Pivotal ANTLER-3 Phase 3 trial start-up for vispa-cel
catalysts.detail: Following FDA alignment reached May 7, 2026 on trial design (~250-patient randomized, controlled study in CD19-naive, transplant-ineligible 2L LBCL patients who cannot access or are not candidates for autologous CAR-T), Caribou is funding start-up activities for the pivotal ANTLER-3 trial, with cash runway guided into 2H 2027; a formal trial initiation/first-patient-dosed date has not been disclosed.

catalysts.label: Additional CB-010/CB-011 data at a major hematology conference (e.g., ASH)
catalysts.detail: Caribou has a pattern of presenting incremental follow-up data on vispa-cel and CB-011 at major medical conferences, most recently an ANTLER e-poster at the Pan-Pacific Lymphoma Conference in July; further updates are plausible at the ASH Annual Meeting in December 2026, though not yet formally announced.

pulse: Caribou remains in a quiet stretch heading into Q2 2026 earnings, expected around August 11-12 but still not formally confirmed by the company as of July 24, with the EHA 2026 data from June (vispa-cel durability in lymphoma, deep and durable CB-011 responses in myeloma) still the dominant narrative; a smaller ANTLER e-poster at the Pan-Pacific Lymphoma Conference in Hawaii (July 20-24) keeps vispa-cel visible while the pivotal ANTLER-3 trial start-up remains the next concrete milestone to watch after FDA trial-design alignment in May. The stock has firmed to roughly the low-$2 range (market cap near $190 million) after a strong week of gains, and sell-side coverage stays uniformly Strong Buy/Buy, with consensus price targets clustered well above the current share price (roughly $9-$12, including H.C. Wainwright's $11 target reiterated with a Buy rating), reflecting a persistent gap between analyst optimism about the allogeneic CAR-T platform and the market's caution given the company's modest cash position and looming financing needs. Watch items into year-end include CB-011 dose-expansion data, any update on pivotal ANTLER-3 trial start-up timing, and the company's cash-runway guidance (last reported at $118.6M, into 2H 2027) at its next earnings report.
